channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nunavut prepares for next year’s transfer of land and resource control from Ottawa
item.description: Nunavut is entering its final year before devolution takes full effect in April 2027, when responsibility for managing the territory’s land and natural resources will transfer from the federal governm...

item.title: Philippines Requests Safe Passage for Its Ships from Iran Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: The Philippines has formally requested Iran to recognize it as a “non-hostile country” and to guarantee safe passage for Philippine-flagged vessels and oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. This...

item.title: The Role of Russia, India, and China in Shaping Asia’s Emerging Multipolar Order
item.description: The global system is now passing a turning point where the unipolar dominance that once existed has ended, giving way to a fluid and often unstable multipolar world. In this shifting environment, the ...

item.title: NHAI Implements Full Digital Payment System at Toll Plazas Starting 1 April
item.description: From 1 April onwards, the National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has announced that all toll plazas across the country will completely shift to digital payments, ending the use of cash. This majo...

item.title: Akpabio Hails Tinubu at 74, Calls Him Bold and Visionary Leader
item.description: The President of the Nigerian Senate, Godswill Akpabio, don show plenty respect to President Bola Ahmed Tinubu as the President clock 74 years old. For him message wey he personally sign, Akpabio call...

item.title: Top 10 Most Valuable Footballers Who Haven’t Played in a World Cup Yet
item.description: According to Transfermarkt, Norwegian striker Erling Haaland from Manchester City dey top the list of most valuable football players wey never feature in a FIFA World Cup, with him market value pegged...

item.title: Authorities Plan to Remove Deceased Voters From Aadhaar Records
item.description: The Supreme Court has prompted the Election Commission and the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) to take measures to ensure that the electoral rolls and Aadhaar database are accurate an...

item.title: Concerns and Uncertainties Surround Federal Public Servants' Early Retirement Option
item.description: Federal public servants in Canada continue to face uncertainty regarding an early retirement option recently introduced. Questions have arisen about eligibility criteria, long-term pension impacts, an...

item.title: King Charles III to Address US Congress During Official State Visit
item.description: His Majesty King Charles III has been formally invited to address a Joint Meeting of the United States Congress during an upcoming state visit. The invitation was extended by Speaker Mike Johnson, who...

item.title: Chicken Licken ends use of signature rubber bands, customers express concern
item.description: Fast-food chain Chicken Licken has officially announced that it will no longer be using its iconic rubber bands across all branches. These rubber bands, which have long been part of the brand’s packag...

item.title: Kenya Investigates Ray-Ban Meta Smart Glasses over Privacy Concerns
item.description: Kenya’s Office of the Data Protection Commissioner (ODPC) has begun investigations into Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses amid concerns they could secretly record people and misuse personal data. The probe f...

item.title: Overcrowding and Rising Violence Challenge Northern Ontario Jails
item.description: Correctional workers in northern Ontario report that rising violence within jails is being exacerbated by overcrowding, leaving little room to separate inmates after incidents. In many cases, the vict...

item.title: Albanese prepares Australians for economic strain amid the Iran war and global fuel crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese addressed the nation on Wednesday night, urging Australians to maintain normal routines over Easter but warning of tough months ahead as the global economy reels from t...

item.title: Trevor Lawrence: Earnings, Career, and Lifestyle Details in 2026
item.description: Trevor Lawrence, the American NFL quarterback for the Jacksonville Jaguars, has an estimated net worth of $30 million as of 2026. His wealth comes from NFL contracts, endorsements, and his college foo...

item.title: Daily Superquiz and Mini Crossword Challenge
item.description: The Sydney Morning Herald's Superquiz for Thursday, April 2, 2026, invites readers to participate in a daily quiz and a Mini Crossword challenge. The Mini Crossword features ten clues across twenty-fi...

item.title: Salt River Informal Settlement Residents Face Relocation for Social Housing Development
item.description: Residents of an informal settlement located at the former horse stables next to Salt River Market in Cape Town have been given a two-week notice by the City of Cape Town to vacate the property. The si...

item.title: Jamaican woman convicted in stepson’s death granted temporary stay of deportation from Canada
item.description: Nichelle Nikiss Rowe, a Jamaican national who obtained permanent resident status in Canada in 2005, has been granted a temporary reprieve from deportation. Rowe was convicted of second-degree murder i...

item.title: Evolution Petroleum Stock Breaches 50-Day Moving Average
item.description: Evolution Petroleum Corporation (NYSEAmerican:EPM) recently saw its stock price cross above its fifty-day moving average, a technical indicator often watched by investors. This crossover signals a pot...

item.title: How Kenya Can Learn from DRC’s World Cup Success Through Diaspora Recruitment
item.description: Kenya can draw important lessons from the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) after the Leopards secured a historic spot at the 2026 Fifa World Cup. Despite domestic league and infrastructure challenge...

item.title: Progressives Reflect on the Concept of 'Woke 2' Amid Political Turmoil
item.description: Following Donald Trump's 2024 presidential victory, conservatives celebrated what they saw as the definitive end of 'woke' culture, a term originally rooted in awareness of social injustices. Over tim...
